name: Cañada del Horno and Sierra de Gredos Loop
name_original: Puerto de la Cañada del Horno y Valle de Navalacruz — circular por los pies de Sierra de Gredos
region: San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche
description: This difficult 47.8-mile road cycling route in Sierra de Gredos features challenging climbs and stunning mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche traverse the foothills and passes of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with routes often leading through high-altitude terrain and offering views of the surrounding peaks. Road surfaces vary, including well-maintained roads and sections with more challenging gradients. Best road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche The most popular road cycling route is Plataforma de Gredos Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This route features moderate climbs and views towards the Gredos mountain range. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cañada del Horno and Sierra de Gredos Loop, a difficult 47.8 miles (76.9 km) path. This route includes sustained ascents and descents through mountainous terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Chía Pass (1,663 m) – Peña Negra Pass loop from San Martín de la Vega del Alberche, a 54.1 miles (87.1 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche is defined by mountain passes, high-altitude roads, and views of the Sierra de Gredos. The network offers options for easy road bike routes as well as more advanced road cycling routes. The routes in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche, catering to various skill levels. The region is known for its mountain passes and high-altitude roads.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. For a moderate option with beautiful views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316655058/plataforma-de-gredos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plataforma de Gredos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.6 miles (33.1 km) long.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche?
A: The region is ideal for advanced road cyclists, with 13 difficult routes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315465827/canada-del-horno-and-sierra-de-gredos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada del Horno and Sierra de Gredos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.8-mile (76.9 km) path with sustained ascents and descents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the typical length of road cycling routes in this region?
A: Route lengths vary significantly. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316655058/plataforma-de-gredos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plataforma de Gredos Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 20.6 miles (33.1 km), up to longer, more demanding routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m) – Peña Negra Pass loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 54.1 miles (87.1 km).

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316655058/plataforma-de-gredos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plataforma de Gredos Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m) – Peña Negra Pass loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which routes offer the best scenic viewpoints of the Sierra de Gredos?
A: Many routes provide stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19434723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Gredos mountain range – Sierra de Gredos loop from Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for this, offering panoramic vistas. You can also find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; highlight along various paths.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5758520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5754260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Paredes&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6213569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5912440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Tormes River and Dolmen&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural monument.

Q: Are there any notable mountain passes to explore by road bike?
A: San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche is characterized by its mountain passes. Cyclists frequently traverse the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5216726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1711130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Cañada del Horno&lt;/a&gt;, all offering challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging mountain passes, the high-altitude roads, and the stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in San Martín De La Vega Del Alberche?
A: Given the region's high-altitude terrain and mountain passes, the best seasons for road biking are typically late spring, summer, and early autumn. During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for cycling.

Q: Are there routes that start from Hoyos del Espino?
A: Yes, several routes are accessible from Hoyos del Espino. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19434723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Gredos mountain range – Sierra de Gredos loop from Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33755520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos del Espino – Peña Negra Pass loop from Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt; both begin in this location.
tips: It is 1,767 meters above sea level and connects the Alto Alberche valley with that of Alto Tormes, through a local road that connects the towns of San Martín de la Vega del Alberche and Barajas, annexed to Navarredonda de Gredos, in whose municipal area it is located. located the coronation of the same.
Perfect Area for a little break

Beautiful and at the same time small town that is characterized by the Sierra de la Serrota and to the north, by the "dehesas" that are close to the Río Alberche (Dehesa de San Martín and Dehesa de las Cañadas).

faqs.question: How difficult is the Puerto de la Cañada del Horno y Valle de Navalacruz cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for road cycling. It features a significant climb to the Alto de la Cañada del Horno, reaching 1760 meters, with gradients often exceeding 5-6% and double-digit ramps in the final kilometer. Strong winds can also add to the challenge.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification, significant elevation gain (1360m), and challenging climbs, this route is generally not recommended for beginner road cyclists. It's better suited for experienced riders looking for a demanding and scenic challenge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 76.9 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 55 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the tranquil and beautiful landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos foothills, including stunning mountain views, chestnut and oak forests, and charming mountain villages. In June, the area is particularly vibrant with the yellow bloom of native mountain broom flowers.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, notable points include the challenging climb to Puerto de la Cañada del Horno, sections of nice calm street and beautiful landscape, and the charming settlement of Cepeda la Mora.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gredos region is beautiful in spring and early summer, especially in June when the mountain broom flowers bloom. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors. However, be prepared for strong winds, which can occur at any time due to the open nature of the area.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private property.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de la Cañada del Horno cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Gredos region, this is a road cycling route. Bringing a dog would typically require them to be in a trailer or basket, which adds significant difficulty to an already challenging ride. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, and adhere to leash laws in any villages or populated areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and passes through several villages. You can typically find parking in towns like Navalacruz or other nearby settlements in the Sierra de Gredos foothills to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas in these villages.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other notable cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 32)", "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 33)", and "[CIMA AV14] La Erilla".

faqs.question: What unique aspects should I be aware of when cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gredos is a renowned cycling destination, often used as a training ground by professional cyclists due to its demanding routes. The Alto de la Cañada del Horno, a key part of this route, can be linked with other significant climbs like Chía Norte and the Plataforma de Gredos for those seeking an even greater challenge. Be prepared for potentially strong winds, especially at higher elevations.
